title: 'Squid Game 3' Sets Netflix Record With Massive 60.1 Million Views in Three Days
text: The Korean drama's third and final season achieved a major streaming milestone. According to Netflix's weekly Top 10 data, the show garnered 60.1 million views during its opening three-day window following its Friday release. This performance surpassed the viewership of Season 2, which drew 68 million views over four days when it debuted on December 26, 2024. The different timeframe matters—Season 2 benefited from an extra day of tracking since it launched on Thursday rather than Friday. The series ranked as Netflix's ninth most-watched non-English language television series overall. By comparison, the popular series Wednesday had previously set a record with 50.1 million views during its 2022 premiere week. Season 3 follows protagonist Gi-hun as he continues through the deadly competition while attempting to challenge the games' organizers.
